meta.title: VipaBase — Vipassana Course Tracker
meta.description: Personal app to register and organize your Vipassana meditation courses. Save dates, locations, teachers, and duration of each sit and serve.
meta.ogDescription: Personal app to register and organize your Vipassana meditation courses.

meta.title: Help — VipaBase
meta.description: VipaBase user guide. Learn how to log Vipassana courses, maintain your daily streak, and convert Excel or Word data to VipaBase format.

HelpPage.title: Help
HelpPage.subtitle: Everything you need to know about VipaBase: from your first course to data export.

intro.title: Introduction
intro.intro: VipaBase is your personal Vipassana meditation course tracker. Log every sit or serve course you take and keep everything organized in one place.
intro.item1: Register courses with date, place, teacher, mode and duration.
intro.item2: View your full history with a filterable, sortable table.
intro.item4: Export and import data as JSON, or migrate from Excel or Word.
intro.item5: Everything synced to the cloud with your account.

courses.title: Registering a course
courses.step2: Go to the Courses page from the dashboard sidebar menu.
courses.step3: Fill in the course start date using the date picker.
courses.step4: Select the country where the course took place. If it doesn't appear, start typing and it will be added automatically.
courses.step5: Select the venue — only centers in your chosen country are shown. You can also type a new one.
courses.step7: Choose the mode: sit or serve.
courses.step8: Enter how many days the course lasted.
courses.step9: Add notes or comments if you like (optional).
courses.step10: Click Save and the course will appear in your dashboard automatically.

faq.title: FAQ
faq.q1: Can I edit or delete a course after saving it?
faq.a1: Yes. In the dashboard table you'll find Edit (pencil icon) and Delete (trash icon) buttons on the right side of each row. Editing lets you change any field; deleting removes it permanently.
faq.q2: Can I have two courses on the same date?
faq.a2: Yes. VipaBase allows duplicate dates. If you took two parallel courses (one sit and one serve, for example), just log them separately.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.intro: The dashboard gives you an overview of your practice and access to all features.
dashboard.item1: Summary cards: total courses, days sitting, days serving, countries visited.
dashboard.item2: Courses table: sort by any column, search by text, filter by mode.
dashboard.item3: "Download JSON" button: export all your courses as a JSON file.
dashboard.item4: "Import JSON" button: load courses from a previously exported JSON file.

racha.title: Daily meditation streak
racha.intro: The Daily Practice page lets you track your meditation sessions day by day and build the habit.
racha.item1: Each streak has a start date and an end date.
racha.item2: Active streaks have no end date and are marked with the "active" badge.
racha.item3: You can only have one active streak at a time. Starting a new one automatically closes the previous one.
racha.item4: Use the DatePicker to select the exact day, month and year.

chatgpt.title: Convert Excel or Word with ChatGPT
chatgpt.intro: If you have courses logged in Excel, Word, Google Sheets, Notion or any other format, use ChatGPT to convert them to VipaBase JSON format.
chatgpt.step1: Copy the content from your document (Excel table, Word list, whatever format).
chatgpt.step3: Paste this exact prompt (replace ONLY the fields in [brackets]):
chatgpt.step4: Paste your document content below the prompt and send it.
chatgpt.step5: ChatGPT will return a JSON. Copy that JSON to your clipboard.
chatgpt.step6: Go back to VipaBase, navigate to the Courses page, and use the "Import JSON" button.
chatgpt.tip: Before you start, make sure the dates in your document use a consistent format (e.g., YYYY-MM-DD or DD/MM/YYYY) to avoid conversion errors.
chatgpt.prompt:
I'll give you a list of Vipassana meditation courses I've taken, in various formats. I need you to convert them into a JSON array with this exact structure, nothing else:

[{
  "start_date": "YYYY-MM-DD",
  "teacher": "Teacher name",
  "country": "Country",
  "place": "Center name",
  "mode": "sit" or "serve",
  "days": NUMBER,
  "obs": "Optional notes"
}]

Rules:
- start_date in YYYY-MM-DD format
- mode: "sit" for sitting courses, "serve" for service courses
- days: number only
- If a field is not available, use null

Here are my records:

export.title: Exporting and importing data
export.intro: VipaBase lets you download all your courses as JSON and re-import them whenever you need.
export.step1: On the dashboard, click "Download JSON". A courses.json file will be downloaded.
export.step2: To import, go to the Courses page and use the "Import JSON" button. Select the file and courses will be loaded automatically.
export.step3: You can also convert data from Excel, Word or other formats using ChatGPT (see previous section).
